messages.author: BenCos18
messages.text: yep got it I think just before you replied lol alias: Turn on WLED on MeshCore direct message mode: single triggers: - event_type: meshcore_message event_data: sender_name: BCOS message: pc message_type: direct trigger: event conditions: - condition: template value_template: | {{ trigger.event.data.pubkey_prefix == 'c55b6b7f0ebc' }} actions: - data: name: MeshCore Trigger message: Direct message 'pc' received from BCOS action: logbook.log - target: entity_id: light.wled action: light.toggle data: {} should have probly asked before going reverse enginnering stuff edited as this works better lol (edited)
messages.content_html: yep got it I think just before you replied lol alias: Turn on WLED on MeshCore direct message mode: single triggers: - event_type: meshcore_message event_data: sender_name: BCOS message: pc message_type: direct trigger: event conditions: - condition: template value_template: | {{ trigger.event.data.pubkey_prefix == 'c55b6b7f0ebc' }} actions: - data: name: MeshCore Trigger message: Direct message 'pc' received from BCOS action: logbook.log - target: entity_id: light.wled action: light.toggle data: {} should have probly asked before going reverse enginnering stuff edited as this works better lol (edited)

messages.author: mannkind
messages.text: I use the MeshCore integration in HA w/a BT Proxy. This is my current patch to MeshCore. It disables the pin if it is set to the default PIN. Long ago when I was pairing my MT client and an RPI CM4, I had to pair it manually first via the CLI before I could actually use it in my scripts. (edited)

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: This is a way to integrate private messages (PM) with public and other channels in a tabbed card. It doesn't use any additional entities, the number of messages is unlimited, and they last for the lifetime of your logbook. This is accomplished through a small automation. The how-to can be found at: https://github.com/WJ4IoT/Meshcore-Home-Assistant-Solutions/tree/main

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: Easiest nodes are ESP32 starting with Heltec V3 because this one is already prepared for Wifi, next Heltec WSL similar to V3 (start from ble; comment ble lines and add wifi lines from V3), Benefit WSL is the external Wifi connector for improved Wifi reception with better antenna. Benefit ESP32 over nRF more memory for contacts.

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: Not sure if this is helpful in your situation (also running everything in Docker) but with multiple USB attached to your system might swap USB ports on reboot. This is the remark I made in my compose.yml how to prevent this: # Use for USB device its ID and not the assigned USB port because when there are more than 1 USB devices attached on a # system reboot assigned USB ports can (and will) change. UDEV can be used also but requires extra work outside the container. # Command to show USB devices by ID: sudo ls -la /dev/serial/by-id # for your docker-container use a logical USB name. /dev/serial/by-id/usb-FTDI_FT232R_USB_UART_AK2NEAM6-if00-port0:/dev/ttyUSB_DSMR
messages.content_html:
Not sure if this is helpful in your situation (also running everything in Docker) but with multiple USB attached to your system might swap USB ports on reboot. This is the remark I made in my compose.yml how to prevent this: # Use for USB device its ID and not the assigned USB port because when there are more than 1 USB devices attached on a # system reboot assigned USB ports can (and will) change. UDEV can be used also but requires extra work outside the container. # Command to show USB devices by ID: sudo ls -la /dev/serial/by-id # for your docker-container use a logical USB name.
  • /dev/serial/by-id/usb-FTDI_FT232R_USB_UART_AK2NEAM6-if00-port0:/dev/ttyUSB_DSMR

messages.author: mueslimaker
messages.text: No, I mean the messages from the companion node itself, which in this case would be the responses from a command (such as OK, or the value of a query). For contacts such as a repeater, they have a logbook entity where those responses show up. But there isn't one for the companion node itself (the one that's paired to HA)
messages.content_html: No, I mean the messages from the companion node itself, which in this case would be the responses from a command (such as OK, or the value of a query). messages.author: mueslimaker
messages.text: Something I noticed too with the HA integration is that with some commands the methods in meshcore_py require that parameters by of type bytes, and the HA integration just sends strings. For instance the set_channel method takes the channel key as bytes, and has no logic for handling conversion from string to bytes. When I send this command from HA it just says that it’s missing a required parameter

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: BTW I do not try to combine a message with a path, also SNR is only important if you have no own repeater in this respect. But from each contact I try to save 'fastest' path, distance, last advert and last heard, this latter because companions tents to forgets to do an advert and the integration shows them quickly as stale.
messages.content_html: BTW I do not try to combine a message with a path, also SNR is only important if you have no own repeater in this respect. messages.author: mueslimaker
messages.text: I’ve found that using WiFi is the reliable method for hooking to home assistant. When I tried using USB I got disconnections like you’re describing Compiling WiFi firmware for the heltec was super easy. You just enable the packet logging flag in the platformio config file for the heltec platform, and I just edited build.sh so it only built the wanted heltec repeater WiFi firmware instead of all of them
messages.content_html: I’ve found that using WiFi is the reliable method for hooking to home assistant. When I tried using USB I got disconnections like you’re describing Compiling WiFi firmware for the heltec was super easy. You just enable the packet logging flag in the platformio config file for the heltec platform, and I just edited build.sh so it only built the wanted heltec repeater WiFi firmware instead of all of them

messages.author: MrAlders0n
messages.text: This automation listens for any public message on channel 0 and checks if the sender has already been seen, either by matching an advert/contact entity or by being listed in the known-senders text helper (with a 3000-character limit). If the sender is new, it adds them to the helper so they won’t trigger again, sends a short welcome message with links, and also notifies me of the new node.
messages.content_html: This automation listens for any public message on channel 0 and checks if the sender has already been seen, either by matching an advert/contact entity or by being listed in the known-senders text helper (with a 3000-character limit). If the sender is new, it adds them to the helper so they won’t trigger again, sends a short welcome message with links, and also notifies me of the new node.

Hey - could you possibly share this automation with me? Thank you

messages.author: MrAlders0n
messages.text: alias: MeshCore - Messages from New (Unknown) Node description: >- Notify once when a node on ch0 speaks but no contact entity exists and sender not in known list triggers: - event_type: meshcore_message event_data: message_type: channel channel_idx: 0 trigger: event conditions: - condition: template value_template: >- {% set sender = trigger.event.data.sender_name | trim %} {% set matches = states.binary_sensor | selectattr('entity_id','search','^binary_sensor\\.meshcore_.*_contact$') | selectattr('attributes.adv_name','defined') | selectattr('attributes.adv_name','equalto', sender) | list %} {% set seen = states('input_text.meshcore_known_senders') | from_json(default=[]) %} {{ (matches | length) == 0 and sender not in seen }} actions: - variables: _sender: "{{ trigger.event.data.sender_name | trim }}" _seen: >- {{ states('input_text.meshcore_known_senders') | from_json(default=[]) }} _new_seen_json: "{{ (_seen + [_sender]) | unique | list | tojson }}" - target: entity_id: input_text.meshcore_known_senders data: value: "{{ _new_seen_json }}" action: input_text.set_value - data: channel_idx: 0 message: >- Welcome @[{{ _sender }}] to Ottawa MeshCore! Check out https://ottawamesh.ca and join our Discord (link on the wiki). :wave: action: meshcore.send_channel_message enabled: true - delay: hours: 0 minutes: 0 seconds: 2 milliseconds: 0 - data: channel_idx: 0 message: "@[{{ _sender }}] Join #ottawa + #testing. " action: meshcore.send_channel_message enabled: true mode: queued max: 10 (edited)

messages.author: drewzh
messages.text: Just a PSA that after switching to TCP from USB, all of my drop-out issues are resolved. I actually think the issue is caused by Unraid/Docker locking up the device when a forward is defined - if I shut down the HA container, connect the node and then spin it back up again, it seems to resolve the USB issue for a while
messages.content_html: Just a PSA that after switching to TCP from USB, all of my drop-out issues are resolved. I actually think the issue is caused by Unraid/Docker locking up the device when a forward is defined - if I shut down the HA container, connect the node and then spin it back up again, it seems to resolve the USB issue for a while

messages.author: MrAlders0n
messages.text: At some point, I’ll post all my automations on GitHub for the public once they’re cleaned up and tested. For now—we’re in love with this project. Thanks @awol for all your work on this! We currently use it for: New companion speaks in public: Auto-replies in public, shares our Discord and pubic channels, and logs the companion’s name in a JSON list the first time they speak to ensure it never sends the welcome message again. New repeater advert: Sends our Discord channel the repeater name, public key, and lat/lon. It also checks its internal JSON database for overlapping IDs. Same logic as above—if the public key isn’t already in the known list, it triggers. Bot commands: dmme : Auto DMs the user 10 times (testing). ping : Responds with “pong” in public. weather : Gives a short forecast for today and tomorrow in public. channels : Lists the public channels we use. (edited)
messages.content_html:
At some point, I’ll post all my automations on GitHub for the public once they’re cleaned up and tested. For now—we’re in love with this project. Thanks @awol for all your work on this! We currently use it for:
  • New companion speaks in public:
Auto-replies in public, shares our Discord and pubic channels, and logs the companion’s name in a JSON list the first time they speak to ensure it never sends the welcome message again.
  • New repeater advert:
Sends our Discord channel the repeater name, public key, and lat/lon. It also checks its internal JSON database for overlapping IDs. Same logic as above—if the public key isn’t already in the known list, it triggers.
  • Bot commands:
  • dmme: Auto DMs the user 10 times (testing).
  • ping: Responds with “pong” in public.
  • weather: Gives a short forecast for today and tomorrow in public.
  • channels: Lists the public channels we use.
 (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 2

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: The number of hops is available in the message received as "path_len" in both "EventType.CHANNEL_MSG_RECV" as "EventType.CONTACT_MSG_RECV", you also have SNR but that is from the last repeater which is often your own thus not really important but from RAW you can extract a grah from your direct repeaters which give a good impression of conditions. (edited)
messages.content_html: The number of hops is available in the message received as "path_len" in both "EventType.CHANNEL_MSG_RECV" as "EventType.CONTACT_MSG_RECV", you also have SNR but that is from the last repeater which is often your own thus not really important but from RAW you can extract a grah from your direct repeaters which give a good impression of conditions. (edited)

messages.reply_html:
Wouter-Jan
The number of hops is available in the message received as "path_len" in both "EventType.CHANNEL_MSG_RECV" as "EventType.CONTACT_MSG_RECV", you also have SNR but that is from the last repeater which is often your own thus not really important but from RAW you can extract a grah from your direct repeaters which give a good impression of conditions. (edited)

messages.author: dotdavid
messages.text: Hi, posted on #development and was sent here. I have a device attached to my homeassistant at home which is working well. However I want to see the traffic where I work too so used TailScale and socat to expose another device connected to a remote Raspberry Pi to the Tailnet. Home Assistant connects ok to that device via TCP (well, with some timeouts, but I'm looking into that) but then Home Assistant's Meshcore integration starts getting very confused, with entities from my home network disappearing and generally the UI acting odd until I disable the second device. I believe there is an assumption in the code that there is only going to be one meshcore radio attached to home assistant - is this the case? (edited)

messages.author: MrAlders0n
messages.text: How the MeshCore Ping Works When any packet hits the node, Home Assistant fires a meshcore_raw_event with the RX LOG — that’s the raw LoRa frame before decryption. My automation decodes that log in real time: Parses the header , path length , and path bytes (each hop’s repeater ID prefix). Checks the channel-hash byte — if it’s 0x11 , that means it’s the Public channel . Stores the timestamp, hop count, and path string into HA input helpers ( meshcore_last_* ). I only have it respond to ping in the public channel. Later, when someone sends(only in public ): @[Bender] !ping and that channel message arrives ( EventType.CHANNEL_MSG_RECV ), Home Assistant compares its timestamp with the most recent RX LOG entry. If they match within a few seconds and the path lengths line up, Bender replies automatically: Pong — your ping was received in 3 hops • Path: CF→92→4E So the bot’s reply actually reflects the path in the RXLOG it’s possible it could be wrong and grab the wrong packet of course if enough come in at the same time (edited)
messages.content_html:
How the MeshCore Ping Works When any packet hits the node, Home Assistant fires a meshcore_raw_event with the RX LOG — that’s the raw LoRa frame before decryption. My automation decodes that log in real time:
  • Parses the header, path length, and path bytes (each hop’s repeater ID prefix).
  • Checks the channel-hash byte — if it’s 0x11, that means it’s the Public channel.
  • Stores the timestamp, hop count, and path string into HA input helpers (meshcore_last_*). I only have it respond to ping in the public channel. Later, when someone sends(only in public ):
@[Bender] !ping and that channel message arrives (EventType.CHANNEL_MSG_RECV), Home Assistant compares its timestamp with the most recent RX LOG entry. If they match within a few seconds and the path lengths line up, Bender replies automatically:
Pong — your ping was received in 3 hops • Path: CF→92→4E
So the bot’s reply actually reflects the path in the RXLOG it’s possible it could be wrong and grab the wrong packet of course if enough come in at the same time (edited)
messages.reply_html:
Wouter-Jan
Your companion sees only 1 path? Could be more, or you just take the first because that is the fastest? Based on the timestamp? Maybe you can share your work in progress so others will be inspired by your example and we get sort of cross-pollination of solutions.

messages.text: I've reproduced the issue. It works fine if there are two integration entries, and if either one of them is the only one monitoring a repeater station. However if both integration entities are monitoring a repeater station it seems that one of them loses all of its monitored entities in the UI except in the "Managed Monitored Devices" radio button option in the main MeshCore integration page that you get by clicking the gear icon next to each integration entity. I tried looking at the code but I'm a bit baffled. I assume it's something to do with database keys or something and I do get messages saying "Platform meshcore does not generate unique IDs" in the log for the tracked repeaters. But looking at the code I can't really understand why their IDs wouldn't be unique. Can anyone reproduce this? (edited)

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: There are many solutions, this is yet another one. Background when WhatsApp integration failed me after returning from holiday I tried to implement a Meshcore - Telegram connection (one-way street). Objective was to publish public messages and adverts in telegram Including map, distance and path details. The first was also recently created (by a friend) in a web-version (with MQTT and node-red) and with (to be honest) a better layout. The second could be a good alternative for ping pong because no RF is used to reply and it stimulates people to do a companion Advert Flood more often (but hopefully not to often). Is it new? Not really I am reusing stuff already published in my https://discord.com/channels/1343693475589263471/1358879320248160419/1417781335787049020 How does it look like in Telegram? See: https://t.me/+ep-R777LtC9hMjU0 (changed link to invite) PS Does anyone know how to automate to copy a graph from Home Assistant and save it as image to be published in Telegram (or WhatsApp)? (edited)

messages.author: Liam Cottle
messages.text: I originally had a fixed timeout of 30 seconds in the app for fetching all contacts, but even had issues increasing it. some devices were just really slow at pushing packets. I ended up switching to a new method, where I have an initial timeout of 10 seconds, and it's reset to 10 seconds each time a contact is received. that way fetching contacts can force timeout after 10 seconds of getting nothing. this continues to work even as contact limit is increased, because you no longer have to care how long it takes to fetch all contacts, only how long it takes to fetch 1 (edited)
messages.content_html: I originally had a fixed timeout of 30 seconds in the app for fetching all contacts, but even had issues increasing it. some devices were just really slow at pushing packets. I ended up switching to a new method, where I have an initial timeout of 10 seconds, and it's reset to 10 seconds each time a contact is received. that way fetching contacts can force timeout after 10 seconds of getting nothing. this continues to work even as contact limit is increased, because you no longer have to care how long it takes to fetch all contacts, only how long it takes to fetch 1 (edited)

messages.text: so then the retries shouldnt have accounted for all of the traffic if I read that correctly?
messages.content_html: so then the retries shouldnt have accounted for all of the traffic if I read that correctly?
messages.reply_html:
awol
@Jade @Cisien retries happen with an exponential back off spread out evenly through the refresh interval. The backoff delay is actually calculated backwards from the refresh interval so if you have a 12hr interval the delay for each retry (capped at 5) should be: 696 s → 11m 36s 1392 s → 23m 12s 2784 s → 46m 24s 5568 s → 1h 32m 48s 11136 s → 3h 5m 36s

  • delete old meshcore integration (beta wasn't listed when I tried)
  • add integration
  • reboot (not restart HA)
  • add WiFi companion to meshcore integration
  • didn't appear to work, did not find companion
  • reboot
  • add again
  • reboot
  • companion finally showed up
Basically, reboot after you add or delete something every time

messages.author: mattzz
messages.text: Flaky connectivity, devices randomly disappearing etc. points in the direction of power issues. Connecting the device without battery means that USB has only to deliver power to the heltec and not charge a battery at the same time. I suspect the usb port on the device is not capable of delivering much more than 500mA if the HA green is based on an embedded board.

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: if a user-set path request fails, don't reset or forget the path, try flood once. if flood doesn't work, wait for its next turn, and use the original user-set path again. if flood works, note that, and next time the original user-set path fails, try the flood-found path, once. if it fails, wait for the next turn again. try not to flood at all cost, use it only once to see if another path is possible.
messages.content_html: if a user-set path request fails, don't reset or forget the path, try flood once. if flood doesn't work, wait for its next turn, and use the original user-set path again. if flood works, note that, and next time the original user-set path fails, try the flood-found path, once. if it fails, wait for the next turn again. try not to flood at all cost, use it only once to see if another path is possible.

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: Looking in Unifi Network the main difference I can see is that my main SSID has a split config between 2.5 and 5 to allow different settings. 2.4GHz WiFi Optimisation IoT - on Multicast to unicast - off UAPSD - off Minimum Data Rate Control - auto WPA2 PMF Disabled 5Ghz Uses the same SSID and Private Preshared keys with normal Wi-Fi settings. WiFi 7 is a separate SSID with MLO on 5Ghz and 6 GHz only as it needs WPA3, it does not touch 2.4. I'm not sure any of this is needed for the ESP devices but you could try it, I have spent a lot less time looking at disconnect issues since I did it this way. (edited)

messages.author: Jade
messages.text: Is Disable Repeater something I can do in 2.1.8?
messages.content_html: Is Disable Repeater something I can do in 2.1.8?
messages.reply_html:
awol
If you really want to go down the route of more custom automation, you can completely take over the request patterns. If you "Disable repeater" in the config this will keep all the sensors in place but stop automatic requests. Then if you can make an automation that makes requests for repeater stats using this action/service: service: meshcore.execute_command data: command: 'send_statusreq <PUBKEY_PREFIX>' Since the sensor subscribe to all status responses from the node the sensors will update even when you make the request manually via this service.

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: May I do a suggestion for "Clearing Discovered Contacts", the steps can be reduced and simplified. For starters people tents to advice a HA-restart much to quickly. This can be replaced by reloading the specific part. The steps are: Rename the storage file: .storage/meshcore.<entry_id>.discovered_contacts (better safe than sorry, delete later) perform action "homeassistant.reload_config_entry" for Meshcore device
messages.content_html:
May I do a suggestion for "Clearing Discovered Contacts", the steps can be reduced and simplified. For starters people tents to advice a HA-restart much to quickly. This can be replaced by reloading the specific part. The steps are:
  • Rename the storage file: .storage/meshcore.<entry_id>.discovered_contacts (better safe than sorry, delete later)
  • perform action "homeassistant.reload_config_entry" for Meshcore device

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: If you have 300 contacts and more than some are obsolete you can use this automation for convenience. As you can see 'stale' is used but the period is extended with your desired number of days (based upon lastmod and not last_advert because of possible incorrect clock settings on repeaters). Hope you like it.
messages.content_html: If you have 300 contacts and more than some are obsolete you can use this automation for convenience. As you can see 'stale' is used but the period is extended with your desired number of days (based upon lastmod and not last_advert because of possible incorrect clock settings on repeaters). Hope you like it.

messages.author: dotdavid
messages.text: @^mlake I do this as my HA server isn't in easy bluetooth range of my companion device that I use for HA. So I have a pi that exposes the serial port over TCP port 5000 using ser2net, and MC HA (or even the phone app) can then connect to the companion device over TCP.
messages.content_html: @^mlake I do this as my HA server isn't in easy bluetooth range of my companion device that I use for HA. So I have a pi that exposes the serial port over TCP port 5000 using ser2net, and MC HA (or even the phone app) can then connect to the companion device over TCP.

messages.text: For TCP/IP connection the Heltec V3 or WSL3 are best suited because their platformio.ini is already prepared for wifi (maybe there are more). WSL3 needs slightly more work to prepare but have the advantage to connect an external wifi antenna.

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: @^mlake as soon you see it in "view telemetry" it is likely to be pick-up automatic in the MC integration; I have a RAK repeater with RAK1906 sensor and see this sensor in HA; there are flaws; this sensor was supported in Firmware: v1.7.3 (after small tweaks) but not later ones (as far as I know) and not all sensors got the correct description (due to similarity of values)

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: Heltec v4 also has the WiFi variant set up but adding it to Xiao s3 (maybe also c3 c6) probably isn't much work. Start by copying the USB section in ../variants/xiao_s3_wio/platformio.ini [env:Xiao_S3_WIO_companion_radio_usb] rename it to [env:Xiao_S3_WIO_companion_radio_wifi] add the build flags ; -D WIFI_DEBUG_LOGGING=1 -D WIFI_SSID='"yourSSID"' -D WIFI_PWD='"yourPSK"' try a build of the new [env:Xiao_S3_WIO_companion_radio_wifi] (edited)

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: It was more for other people wanting to do this. Once you start making changes you need to be able keep track of those changes every time you download the source, doing it properly by making a completely new WIFI env in that variant makes it easier. Just adding those to USB makes you loose that option as they cannot currently do more than one, so you need to keep going back and commenting them out to use USB instead of just selecting it. Once you have tested this you could do a PR and ask for it adding to the code so others can benefit. (edited)
messages.content_html: It was more for other people wanting to do this. Once you start making changes you need to be able keep track of those changes every time you download the source, doing it properly by making a completely new WIFI env in that variant makes it easier. Just adding those to USB makes you loose that option as they cannot currently do more than one, so you need to keep going back and commenting them out to use USB instead of just selecting it. Once you have tested this you could do a PR and ask for it adding to the code so others can benefit. (edited)

messages.author: Honza
messages.text: yep that's all I changed ➜ MeshCore git:(b92d9bd) ✗ git diff diff --git a/variants/heltec_v3/platformio.ini b/variants/heltec_v3/platformio.ini index d31648b..c1110e2 100644 --- a/variants/heltec_v3/platformio.ini +++ b/variants/heltec_v3/platformio.ini @@ -179,8 +179,8 @@ build_flags = -D MAX_GROUP_CHANNELS=8 -D DISPLAY_CLASS=SSD1306Display -D WIFI_DEBUG_LOGGING=1 - -D WIFI_SSID='"myssid"' - -D WIFI_PWD='"mypwd"' + -D WIFI_SSID='"ssid"' + -D WIFI_PWD='"password"' ; -D MESH_PACKET_LOGGING=1 ; -D MESH_DEBUG=1 build_src_filter = ${Heltec_lora32_v3.build_src_filter} (edited)

messages.text: I explored this a little bit further and the following command works : action: meshcore.execute_command data: command: change_contact_flags '6ec999788fb9' 1 And now comes the but: the value is stored as an alphanumeric value and not as an integer/numeric (you can store anything best visible in states) it is therefor not uploaded to the companion due to this incorrect field it is therefor not persistent; after receiving an advert from the set companion this set value will be lost. a favorite flag sets through in example the Android APP is a correct integers/numeric value and not effected Hope this is just a small change
messages.content_html:
I explored this a little bit further and the following command works: action: meshcore.execute_command data: command: change_contact_flags '6ec999788fb9' 1 And now comes the but:
  • the value is stored as an alphanumeric value and not as an integer/numeric (you can store anything best visible in states)
  • it is therefor not uploaded to the companion due to this incorrect field
  • it is therefor not persistent; after receiving an advert from the set companion this set value will be lost.
  • a favorite flag sets through in example the Android APP is a correct integers/numeric value and not effected
Hope this is just a small change

messages.reply_html:
mrlm
@boychicken71 Are you using vscode on windows or something else? From https://github.com/meshcore-dev/meshcore-ha/issues/83 Here are very brief instructions which may be enough to follow assuming you are using Windows. Install VScode, install PlatformIO inside that. (let it load completely and then restart VScode). Download and extract the code zip file from https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore Open the folder with VScode. (let it load completely). Change the environment (bottom bar) to env:Heltec_v3_companion_radio_wifi (....) (let it load completely). Open variants\heltec_v3\platformio.ini with the editor Find the section for the variant; [env:Heltec_v3_companion_radio_wifi] Find the WiFi build flags -D WIFI_SSID='"myssid"' -D WIFI_PWD='"mypwd"' Set the details for your WiFi network. Close and save the file (with the x) Backup your Meshcore config first using the app (phone, PC or web), this process will not wipe it but sometimes a full erase helps. Connect the device to the PC via the USB-C connector. Close anything else which could interfere with the serial connection such as the Chrome browser if you have used the flasher. Press the upload (->) button on the bottom bar, let it compile and then flash the device. Providing nothing has gone wrong you have now converted from BT to WiFi firmware. Back in Meshcore-HA, you use the TCP option when setting up the device but if the IP changes it is going to fail so you need something permanent to identify it. On your router (WiFi/Firewall , ISP provided internet device), you may be able to set a DHCP reservation or hostname which solves this issue. (edited)

Ok, we have the blunt axe method to delete the complete file (works great BTW) but to be honest I love the concept of discovered contacts (read: initial there is no need to add them to the node) and hope for a less radical method.

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: @mrlm I hope you are not collecting nodes (like perhaps in the MT-days). Be clad the integration gives the opportunity to go beyond the maximum number of contacts of the node with this discovery setting. If I look at my contacts overview then I can remove 21% of the repeaters because I have not received an advert in the last 3 days (checking lastmod btw to tackle incorrect clock settings). So maintenance is always a good idea. Companions are more difficult due to the notorious lake of doing adverts timely but here you can play with add to the node and even make them favorite. My question to @awol would be is it possible that the discovery "maintenance" does not rely on the meshcore.<node>.discovered_contacts file but can be done completely inside HA; so manual deleting obsolete discovered contacts with also the possibility to automate this?
messages.content_html: @mrlm I hope you are not collecting nodes (like perhaps in the MT-days). Be clad the integration gives the opportunity to go beyond the maximum number of contacts of the node with this discovery setting. If I look at my contacts overview then I can remove 21% of the repeaters because I have not received an advert in the last 3 days (checking lastmod btw to tackle incorrect clock settings). So maintenance is always a good idea. Companions are more difficult due to the notorious lake of doing adverts timely but here you can play with add to the node and even make them favorite. My question to @awol would be is it possible that the discovery "maintenance" does not rely on the meshcore.<node>.discovered_contacts file but can be done completely inside HA; so manual deleting obsolete discovered contacts with also the possibility to automate this?

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: In the early days I was collecting nodes to see how the mesh was spreading and had them all added as contacts, this eventually got too much for the board to deal with. I have removed them all, cleaning up HA of all traces of the integration and its entities and only having a tiny number of added contacts now. After a few days I have 500 discovered again and it starts to slow other bits of HA down, obviously half the previous 1000 were old or distant nodes, which now cleaned up wont come back. If remove the discovered contacts file occasionally it it doesn't use any resources but leaves behind the contact which I will need to clean up later. I am going to rebuild the whole of HA on Proxmox as it has been running for years and use the PI for something else. (edited)

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: No it does not start as junk because you received an advert and a system does not know if this is a contact you want to keep or not, so if @awol turns it off or create a toggle the consequence is that you will not receive ANY new adverts as a (discovered) contact unless other preparations are made. @mrlm yes as long as they are in the meshcore.<node>.discovered_contacts they swap to discovered state again, but the blunt axe method deleting that file works but this will remove ALL discovered contacts. and yes it would be nice when you change a contact from discovered to stale/fresh it is deleted from this file and if you remove a contact (from your node) it will only return in this file again (and as a discovered contact) after a new advert is received. However this solves not the problem of deleting obsolete discovered contacts (unless with the detour) or the new adverts @AG / Andrew whishes to ignore.
messages.content_html: No it does not start as junk because you received an advert and a system does not know if this is a contact you want to keep or not, so if @awol turns it off or create a toggle the consequence is that you will not receive ANY new adverts as a (discovered) contact unless other preparations are made. @mrlm yes as long as they are in the meshcore.<node>.discovered_contacts they swap to discovered state again, but the blunt axe method deleting that file works but this will remove ALL discovered contacts. and yes it would be nice when you change a contact from discovered to stale/fresh it is deleted from this file and if you remove a contact (from your node) it will only return in this file again (and as a discovered contact) after a new advert is received. However this solves not the problem of deleting obsolete discovered contacts (unless with the detour) or the new adverts @AG / Andrew whishes to ignore.

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: It looks like that the problem might not be completely HA because it looks like that the discovered file resides in memory (roundish 3 in 1 mb); when I delete this file without doing any further action, the file is recreated in its original size. For those who needs to take frequent action and give @awol some room for his changes below an automation to make this easier: alias: Meshcore - Maintenance Delete Discovered Contacts File & Reload description: "" triggers: [] conditions: [] actions: - action: shell_command.delete_discovered_contact_file data: {} - action: homeassistant.reload_config_entry metadata: {} data: {} target: device_id: 97b090d2c09320ecc86f3f564e55345d - action: meshcore.cleanup_unavailable_contacts data: {} mode: single The awful device_id you need to change in your Meshcore device with all the contacts. You also need the shell_command : shell_command: delete_discovered_contact_file: mv -f /config/.storage/meshcore.01K94REZTJ9ANQPD6ZNS4JG8CX.discovered_contacts /config/.storage/meshcore.01K94REZTJ9ANQPD6ZNS4JG8CX.discovered_contacts_old Here you need to change the folder and name of the file into your correct values. f.y.i. I am working with a dockered HA environment so I might skip some parent folder due to the command: volumes: - "/opt/homeassistant/config:/config"` in my Docker file so in your situation addressing this file might be different. The automation was now made complete with the action: meshcore.cleanup_unavailable_contacts to get really rid of them. If you want to go back to the original situation that is possible with the action of two renaming files and one action: homeassistant.reload_config_entry . (so do not use this complete automation) I tested it for my situation and works like a charm (edited)
messages.content_html: It looks like that the problem might not be completely HA because it looks like that the discovered file resides in memory (roundish 3 in 1 mb); when I delete this file without doing any further action, the file is recreated in its original size. For those who needs to take frequent action and give @awol some room for his changes below an automation to make this easier: alias: Meshcore - Maintenance Delete Discovered Contacts File & Reload description: "" triggers: [] conditions: [] actions: - action: shell_command.delete_discovered_contact_file data: {} - action: homeassistant.reload_config_entry metadata: {} data: {} target: device_id: 97b090d2c09320ecc86f3f564e55345d - action: meshcore.cleanup_unavailable_contacts data: {} mode: single The awful device_id you need to change in your Meshcore device with all the contacts. You also need the shell_command: shell_command: delete_discovered_contact_file: mv -f /config/.storage/meshcore.01K94REZTJ9ANQPD6ZNS4JG8CX.discovered_contacts /config/.storage/meshcore.01K94REZTJ9ANQPD6ZNS4JG8CX.discovered_contacts_old Here you need to change the folder and name of the file into your correct values. f.y.i. I am working with a dockered HA environment so I might skip some parent folder due to the command: volumes: - "/opt/homeassistant/config:/config"` in my Docker file so in your situation addressing this file might be different. The automation was now made complete with the action: meshcore.cleanup_unavailable_contacts to get really rid of them. If you want to go back to the original situation that is possible with the action of two renaming files and one action: homeassistant.reload_config_entry. (so do not use this complete automation) I tested it for my situation and works like a charm (edited)

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: I started fresh; so with an empty discovered file, cleanup unavailable contacts, etc. Toggled global function to be sure. When receiving a new contact this contact is added as unavailable with limited details, removing this contact works like a charm (towards the discovered contact file), adding this contact will add a line "added_to_node": true (to the discovered contact file) the node itself is updated (visible in an updated node count) , but due to the limited data available data in HA the contact will remain unavailable.
messages.content_html: I started fresh; so with an empty discovered file, cleanup unavailable contacts, etc. Toggled global function to be sure. When receiving a new contact this contact is added as unavailable with limited details, removing this contact works like a charm (towards the discovered contact file), adding this contact will add a line "added_to_node": true (to the discovered contact file) the node itself is updated (visible in an updated node count) , but due to the limited data available data in HA the contact will remain unavailable.

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: My thought was to only create an entity for added contacts and avoid using entities for discovered entirely. As they are not something that gets interacted with all the time they don't need to be loaded in memory or actively being processed. Just use the file or multiple as a simple DB, that way it can scale as needed without affecting performance. (edited)

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: Is this happens first do a reload of your node device ID (or a blunt HA restart), for those who are remaining you might have a mismatch between your node and HA, if they still exist in the discovered contact file I prefer in this situation for all those unavailable records a remove_contact over remove_discovered_contact to eliminate this mismatch. Last step is cleanup unavailable contacts . If you still have a mismatch between your node count and contacts (difference +1 is ok) count a new advert of those records might solve it, but others are perhaps already inactive. I solved this outside HA with the Android APP. . Explanation: the contacts on your node will always be in HA see meshcore-dev.github.io/meshcore-ha/docs/ha/contacts#automatic-contact-syncing) . (edited)
messages.content_html: Is this happens first do a reload of your node device ID (or a blunt HA restart), for those who are remaining you might have a mismatch between your node and HA, if they still exist in the discovered contact file I prefer in this situation for all those unavailable records a remove_contact over remove_discovered_contact to eliminate this mismatch. Last step is cleanup unavailable contacts. If you still have a mismatch between your node count and contacts (difference +1 is ok) count a new advert of those records might solve it, but others are perhaps already inactive. I solved this outside HA with the Android APP.. Explanation: the contacts on your node will always be in HA see meshcore-dev.github.io/meshcore-ha/docs/ha/contacts#automatic-contact-syncing). (edited)

messages.content_html: I am having issues getting this same config working with ser2net. Would you mind sharing your ser2net.yaml or example for this.
messages.reply_html:
dotdavid
@^mlake I do this as my HA server isn't in easy bluetooth range of my companion device that I use for HA. So I have a pi that exposes the serial port over TCP port 5000 using ser2net, and MC HA (or even the phone app) can then connect to the companion device over TCP.

messages.author: dotdavid
messages.text: Sure, it took a lot of fiddling around. Here's one of my device definitions connection: &dotdavidwork accepter: tcp(nodelay),5000 enable: on timeout: 0 options: kickolduser: true connector: serialdev, /dev/serial/by-id/usb-Silicon_Labs_CP2102_USB_to_UART_Bridge_Controller_0001-if00-port0, 115200n81,local
messages.content_html: Sure, it took a lot of fiddling around. Here's one of my device definitions connection: &dotdavidwork accepter: tcp(nodelay),5000 enable: on timeout: 0 options: kickolduser: true connector: serialdev, /dev/serial/by-id/usb-Silicon_Labs_CP2102_USB_to_UART_Bridge_Controller_0001-if00-port0, 115200n81,local

messages.author: 0xACE
messages.text: Have Meshcore HA up and running, with node(s) and repeater properly added. Have built a "sensor" node that appears on the network and can report telemetry. However the HA Integration seems to only allow adding "client" and "repeater" nodes. The sensor node does not show on the list when I "Add Tracked Client". It does show when "Add Repeater Station" but then there's a bunch of data about repeaters and the telemetry I've added is greyed out and cannot be enabled. I've briefly looked at the source in GitHub. but really cannot figure out how to add a "sensor" node in Meshcore HA integration.
messages.content_html: Have Meshcore HA up and running, with node(s) and repeater properly added. Have built a "sensor" node that appears on the network and can report telemetry. However the HA Integration seems to only allow adding "client" and "repeater" nodes. The sensor node does not show on the list when I "Add Tracked Client". It does show when "Add Repeater Station" but then there's a bunch of data about repeaters and the telemetry I've added is greyed out and cannot be enabled. I've briefly looked at the source in GitHub. but really cannot figure out how to add a "sensor" node in Meshcore HA integration.

GitHub - rgregg/meshcore-proxy: TCP proxy for MeshCore companion ra...
TCP proxy for MeshCore companion radios connected via USB Serial or BLE - rgregg/meshcore-proxy

messages.author: TF-Mambo
messages.text: Explain what you are trying to accomplish. The MC HA integrations allows you to send and receive messages direct or channels. Any device logged into HA would have the same functionality from a single node. Keep in mind the rate limit and that there would be no simultaneous access to the node.
messages.content_html: Explain what you are trying to accomplish. The MC HA integrations allows you to send and receive messages direct or channels. Any device logged into HA would have the same functionality from a single node. Keep in mind the rate limit and that there would be no simultaneous access to the node.
messages.reply_html:
Clinton
Anyone know if it is possible to use a MC node connected to Home Assistant, but expose it for use through other devices? There's this nifty https://github.com/rgregg/meshcore-proxy utility that sure looks like it could do that. I would want it running on my home assistant computer, because it is on all the time, but I've got no idea how I'd run the meshcore-proxy script at bootup time.

messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: The whole point of the proxy is that two applications could use the same node. Suppose Meshcore for Home Assistant sends a few messages every ten minutes; the rest of the time, I could use the node with other software, such as the regular mobile app, or maybe Citadel for MC or anything else. That would be the intent. Use a device for Home Assistant, but also allow it to be used for other purposes in between messages.
messages.content_html: The whole point of the proxy is that two applications could use the same node. Suppose Meshcore for Home Assistant sends a few messages every ten minutes; the rest of the time, I could use the node with other software, such as the regular mobile app, or maybe Citadel for MC or anything else. That would be the intent. Use a device for Home Assistant, but also allow it to be used for other purposes in between messages.
messages.reply_html:
TF-Mambo
Explain what you are trying to accomplish. The MC HA integrations allows you to send and receive messages direct or channels. Any device logged into HA would have the same functionality from a single node. Keep in mind the rate limit and that there would be no simultaneous access to the node.

messages.reply_html:
mrlm
When you connect do you become the primary client, that breaks MC-HA as it receives data all the time in the form of messages and adverts. It would now miss everything while a second client was connected unless there is a clever around this. (edited)

messages.text: Meshcore-HA working with Meshrank.net https://discord.com/channels/1465838297229168925/1465839782188810546/1474056812927127693 Go to Meshrank.net Create account, Login. messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: BT pins don't work properly on HA, you cannot use a proxy and must pair the device first then set up what ever integration you want to use. Disabling the pin requires a custom firmware and quite a few changes. Can you use USB or WiFi instead?
messages.content_html: BT pins don't work properly on HA, you cannot use a proxy and must pair the device first then set up what ever integration you want to use. Disabling the pin requires a custom firmware and quite a few changes. Can you use USB or WiFi instead?

messages.author: PiecesAndPawns
messages.text: I have an idea I'd like to get feedback on, before deciding if it should be raised as a "feature request" via github issues. Since a lot of people use a companion for multiple things (mqtt to letsmesh and other projects, meshcore-bot, home assistant, S.A.M.E weather alerts, and several other things.. it'd be awesome if we could have a single companion node (probably tied to HA), and let the other tools use it as a TCP connection, so that we don't need 11ty7 companions. Would it be possible AND, would it be of interest to create essentially a "tcp proxy" type of service in the HA integration, so the other tools could tie into it and use the same companion?
messages.content_html: I have an idea I'd like to get feedback on, before deciding if it should be raised as a "feature request" via github issues. Would it be possible AND, would it be of interest to create essentially a "tcp proxy" type of service in the HA integration, so the other tools could tie into it and use the same companion?

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: I asked if this proxy idea would be possible at the start but there may be a problem with making it work how people may expect as the firmware is single connection only. Imagine MC-HA is connected and frequently requests the messages, at the same time another device connects in and modifies a contact. How would the responses be routed to the correct requestor? (edited)
messages.content_html: I asked if this proxy idea would be possible at the start but there may be a problem with making it work how people may expect as the firmware is single connection only. Imagine MC-HA is connected and frequently requests the messages, at the same time another device connects in and modifies a contact. How would the responses be routed to the correct requestor? (edited)

messages.author: PiecesAndPawns
messages.text: @mrlm I was thinking more along the lines of HA would have the "direct connection" (BLE, serial, or TCP) to the companion, and expose a TCP port/proxy that other clients could connect to as sort of a "virtual companion", the service can handle sending data to/from the companion, and route the responses to the correct TCP connection
messages.content_html: @mrlm I was thinking more along the lines of HA would have the "direct connection" (BLE, serial, or TCP) to the companion, and expose a TCP port/proxy that other clients could connect to as sort of a "virtual companion", the service can handle sending data to/from the companion, and route the responses to the correct TCP connection

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: Delete it and a blank one will be created next time the integration starts, best to reboot HA though. I would assume it is tagged as corrupt because it is no longer a valid json file which would look something like this when empty. { "version": 1, "minor_version": 1, "key": "meshcore.01xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx.discovered_contacts", "data": { ... } } } Mine is 29500 lines long and I have removed it recently while testing something, dont worry about a few entries. (edited)
messages.content_html: Delete it and a blank one will be created next time the integration starts, best to reboot HA though. I would assume it is tagged as corrupt because it is no longer a valid json file which would look something like this when empty. { "version": 1, "minor_version": 1, "key": "meshcore.01xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx.discovered_contacts", "data": { ... } } } Mine is 29500 lines long and I have removed it recently while testing something, dont worry about a few entries. (edited)

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: HA can do anything... Create an automation - Edit in YAML and paste this very basic one. Save/go back to see the GUI then choose an alternative trigger and channel idx alias: Meshcore - Message ahannel idx1 description: "" triggers: - trigger: time_pattern hours: /1 conditions: [] actions: - action: meshcore.send_channel_message metadata: {} data: channel_idx: 1 message: Test message to Channel idx1 mode: single (edited)

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: I am glad you asked this as I have wanted to do it for ages on my private channel so I can see the route taken when I send a test message. Enable Developer Options in HA, go to Events, meshcore_message and start listening. Send a sample message of the type you want to detect, obviously MC-HA and the node need to be set up to receive this first. Go back to automations and create the new one using the information from the event. alias: Meshcore - reply to a specific message. description: "" triggers: - trigger: event event_type: meshcore_message event_data: message_type: channel channel: "#test" channel_idx: 8 message: MRLM-HA? conditions: [] actions: - action: meshcore.send_channel_message metadata: {} data: channel_idx: 8 message: Reply from MRLM-HA mode: single (edited)

messages.author: mwolter
messages.text: Hi Everyone, Working on a UI for meshcore-ha and would like your input before submitting it to the integration owner for consideration. Basically it’s a nice front end for the integration that makes it easy to manage messages, channels, contacts and your companion and router devices. All existing functionality, entities, logbooks, etc is maintained. It’s a bolt on front end that allows you to continue to use the automations and service for bots or other purposes. It's nice to be able to chat while out of the house and not having to be concerned bad coverage. Let me know if there are features you’re interested in and I’ll look into adding them! (edited)

messages.author: PiecesAndPawns
messages.text: I'm curious about the newer mqtt options. I've got a couple of mqtt servers enabled. I consistently see my node as "offline" in one of the more popular projects, but occasionally see it online. Is it expected that it drops the mqtt connection during periods of mesh inactivity, and reconnects when it hears an advert or something? Or, is this indicative of an issue?
messages.content_html: I'm curious about the newer mqtt options. I've got a couple of mqtt servers enabled. I consistently see my node as "offline" in one of the more popular projects, but occasionally see it online. Is it expected that it drops the mqtt connection during periods of mesh inactivity, and reconnects when it hears an advert or something? Or, is this indicative of an issue?

messages.author: mrlm
messages.text: I have been sending MQTT from MC-HA to • Broker 1: meshrank.net (Enabled) • Broker 2: mqtt.ukmesh.com (Enabled) Both are working reliably as it was the top observer on both sites for ages. The MQTT data is such an insignificant amount compared to other things that it just gets on with it in the background. You clearly have an issue but it must not be how it is configured. Do you have another device you can set up as just an observer, to remove HA temporarilly? Is your ISP and WiFi / LAN stable? Is your HA overloaded? My RPI4-8GB was running HA fine untill MC-HA came along, then I noticed it was occasionally sluggish and the CPU was jumping up to 25% which was suspicious. I discovered that most of the HA / Python code only runs on one core, so the other three were no being used. Moved it to Intel / Proxmox VM and it was back to normal. (edited)
messages.content_html: I have been sending MQTT from MC-HA to • Broker 1: meshrank.net (Enabled) • Broker 2: mqtt.ukmesh.com (Enabled) Both are working reliably as it was the top observer on both sites for ages. The MQTT data is such an insignificant amount compared to other things that it just gets on with it in the background. You clearly have an issue but it must not be how it is configured. Do you have another device you can set up as just an observer, to remove HA temporarilly? Is your ISP and WiFi / LAN stable? Is your HA overloaded? I discovered that most of the HA / Python code only runs on one core, so the other three were no being used. Moved it to Intel / Proxmox VM and it was back to normal. (edited)

Hi Everyone, Working on a UI for meshcore-ha and would like your input before submitting it to the integration owner for consideration. Basically it’s a nice front end for the integration that makes it easy to manage messages, channels, contacts and your companion and router devices. All existing functionality, entities, logbooks, etc is maintained. It’s a bolt on front end that allows you to continue to use the automations and service for bots or other purposes. It's nice to be able to chat while out of the house and not having to be concerned bad coverage. Let me know if there are features you’re interested in and I’ll look into adding them! (edited)
